Title: Root tip excision-induced exodermis lignification impacts lateral root emergence in Brachypodium distachyon

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Moderating C4H activity could tune stress-induced exodermal lignification and thereby improve lateral root emergence and root system architecture plasticity under wounding/stress; deployment should balance barrier formation and defense because lignin reduction may weaken protective outer tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamate to p-coumarate early in phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression of BdCINNAMATE-4-HYDROXYLASE 3 in Bd21-3 is associated with stronger lignin accumulation in the exodermis and reduced lateral root emergence after root tip excision; chemical inhibition of C4H partially rescues emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative time-course RNA-seq; lignin quantification (CASA, thioacidolysis); histochemical staining; confocal Raman spectroscopy; pharmacological inhibition with piperonylic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies early committed flux toward monolignol production
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root tip excision; piperonylic acid treatment; continuous light; 22°C; 1/2 MS medium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after root tip excision, stronger/sustained in Bd21-3 roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: C4H inhibition by piperonylic acid reduced exodermal lignification and partially shifted Bd21-3 from fishbone toward pine-tree phenotype, supporting pathway causality.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing C4H/lignin may compromise mechanical reinforcement, wound response, and barrier properties of outer root tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early control point supported pharmacologically; formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum (expected for C4H; not directly tested here)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdC4H3 was more strongly induced after root tip excision in Bd21-3 than in Bd21. 2. This expression pattern coincided with elevated lignin deposition in the exodermis and delayed lateral root emergence. 3. Chemical inhibition of C4H with piperonylic acid partially rescued the Bd21-3 emergence defect, linking early phenylpropanoid flux to the phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root exodermis and vasculature, especially upper primary root region after excision
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical reinforcement, wound/stress barrier formation, restriction of lateral root emergence when overaccumulated

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PAL genes are candidate levers to tune lignin induction and root barrier rigidity; partial downregulation in outer root tissues may favor lateral root emergence after stress, but whole-plant suppression may broadly alter phenylpropanoid metabolism and defense.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamate, the first step of the phenylpropanoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BdPAL6 is upregulated in Bd21-3 after root tip excision, consistent with increased flux into lignin biosynthesis and stronger exodermal lignification associated with inhibited lateral root emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative time-course RNA-seq linked with lignin staining and biochemical lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies initial precursor flux for monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after root tip excision, especially in Bd21-3 roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed together with BdPAL7 as part of stronger pathway-wide activation in Bd21-3.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lowering PAL activity may reduce defense phenolics and structural integrity beyond lignin effects.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Early committed entry step; key control role implied but not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdPAL6 was induced after root tip excision in Bd21-3. 2. Its induction matches stronger phenylpropanoid flux and higher lignin accumulation in Bd21-3 roots. 3. Elevated PAL expression is associated with exodermal reinforcement and delayed lateral root emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall reinforcement and stress barrier formation

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PAL7 is another candidate for tissue-specific editing to reduce stress-induced lignification and improve lateral root emergence; caution due to broad phenylpropanoid roles.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamate at the entry step of phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BdPAL7 is upregulated in Bd21-3 after root tip excision and likely contributes to elevated lignin accumulation associated with the fishbone phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative time-course RNA-seq linked with lignin phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies initial precursor flux for monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after root tip excision, stronger in Bd21-3
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported with BdPAL6 as induced in Bd21-3.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad reduction could impair defense and structural phenylpropanoids.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Early committed entry step; not formally tested as rate limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdPAL7 is upregulated in Bd21-3 after root tip excision. 2. This induction is part of pathway-wide transcriptional activation toward lignin biosynthesis. 3. The response correlates with increased exodermal lignification and reduced lateral root emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical reinforcement and stress barrier formation

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: 4CL1 is a candidate to fine-tune lignin precursor activation in roots; tissue-specific downregulation may alleviate emergence barriers, but global suppression may weaken lignified tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamates to corresponding CoA thioesters for downstream monolignol synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Bd4-COUMARATE-COA LIGASE 1 shows higher fold-change in Bd21-3 after root tip excision, consistent with enhanced monolignol precursor production and increased lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq with lignin biochemical and microscopy phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Produces activated intermediates required for monolignol branch flux
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: More strongly induced in Bd21-3 after excision
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as one of the key monolignol biosynthetic genes with stronger induction in Bd21-3.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation may alter multiple phenylpropanoid branches, not only lignin.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bd4CL1 was more highly induced in Bd21-3 after root tip excision. 2. Its induction supports increased precursor activation for lignin biosynthesis. 3. The enhanced pathway activity correlates with stronger exodermal lignification and delayed emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall strengthening and barrier formation

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CCR genes are suitable targets to reduce monolignol production in specific root tissues and improve lateral root emergence; major caution is impaired vascular/structural lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters to corresponding cinnamaldehydes in monolignol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BdCCR1-4 showed higher fold-change in Bd21-3 after root tip excision, supporting increased monolignol biosynthesis and lignification associated with reduced lateral root emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative time-course RNA-seq plus lignin biochemical and histological analyses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after excision with stronger induction in Bd21-3
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper groups CCR1-4 rather than functionally dissecting individual isoforms.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced CCR activity may weaken cell walls and stress protection.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key monolignol pathway step; rate limiting not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple BdCCR genes were more strongly induced in Bd21-3 after root tip excision. 2. This supports higher monolignol biosynthetic flux. 3. Elevated CCR expression coincides with stronger exodermal lignification and delayed lateral root emergence.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: COMT3 may be used to alter lignin composition, including S-unit formation, potentially reducing rigid barrier properties if carefully modulated; composition changes may also affect biomechanics unpredictably.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates caffeic acid/5-hydroxyferulate-related intermediates in the pathway toward syringyl-rich monolignols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induction of BdCAFFEIC ACID O-METHYLTRANSFERASE 3 in Bd21-3 indicates increased progression toward monolignol production and altered lignin composition associated with the fishbone phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq with thioacidolysis monomer analysis and lignin microscopy
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Contributes to monolignol composition and precursor conversion toward end-product lignin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after excision, stronger in Bd21-3
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper links elevated S as well as G and H monomer levels to the Bd21-3 response.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing lignin composition can alter stiffness, hydrophobicity, and stress resilience.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Methylated hydroxycinnamate intermediates en route to sinapyl/coniferyl alcohol derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Caffeic acid or related hydroxylated intermediates; S-adenosyl-L-methionine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdCOMT3 induction in Bd21-3 indicates stronger progression toward monolignol production after excision. 2. This correlates with elevated lignin monomer accumulation. 3. Altered composition likely contributes to a more rigid exodermal barrier restricting lateral root emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall reinforcement; affects lignin composition and mechanics

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: F5H5 is a composition-tuning target to modulate syringyl-related lignin features in root barriers; should be combined with flux-control genes and tissue-specific strategies to avoid adverse structural effects.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ferulate/coniferaldehyde-related intermediates toward syringyl monolignol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induction of BdFERULATE 5-HYDROXYLASE 5 in Bd21-3 suggests enhanced progression toward monolignol production and altered lignin composition during the stress response.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq with thioacidolysis and Raman/lignin histochemistry
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after excision, stronger in Bd21-3
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The discussion notes concurrent increase in S-units along with G and H units in Bd21-3.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering S/G balance can affect cell wall mechanics, sealing properties, and stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-determining composition step; rate limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum (expected; not directly tested here)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdF5H5 was induced in Bd21-3 after root tip excision. 2. Its induction indicates enhanced capacity for S-unit-associated monolignol biosynthesis. 3. Together with broader pathway activation, this contributes to altered exodermal lignification associated with delayed lateral root emergence.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BdMYB108 is a candidate regulator for tuning stress-induced exodermal lignification and root system architecture plasticity; ideal use would be tissue/stress-responsive modulation rather than constitutive suppression because lignin-based defense may be beneficial under wounding.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Consistent upregulation of BdMYB108 homologs in Bd21-3 is associated with stronger activation of lignin biosynthetic genes, increased exodermal lignification, and reduced lateral root emergence after root tip excision.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative time-course RNA-seq correlated with lignin pathway activation and lignin phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of wound/stress-induced lignification program in Bd21-3
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root tip excision; putative ABA responsiveness discussed
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively/consistently upregulated in Bd21-3 during the excision response
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a MYB homolog associated with secondary cell wall biosynthesis and likely ABA-responsive.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may affect broader stress responses and secondary wall traits, potentially altering defense or growth.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Lignin biosynthetic genes including PAL, C4H, 4CL, CCR, COMT, F5H modules (inferred from coordinated expression; direct targets not experimentally shown)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively associated with activation of lignin biosynthetic genes in Bd21-3
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (expected for MYB TF; not directly tested here)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdMYB108 homologs were consistently upregulated in Bd21-3 after root tip excision. 2. Their expression pattern parallels stronger induction of lignin biosynthetic genes. 3. This is associated with enhanced exodermal lignification and delayed lateral root emergence.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical reinforcement and stress barrier formation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Mechanical wounding/root tip excision; putative ABA signaling

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BdMYB112 is another candidate for engineering reduced stress-triggered lignification in outer roots to favor branching; deployment should preserve adequate wound/barrier protection.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Consistent upregulation of BdMYB112 homologs in Bd21-3 is associated with stronger lignin pathway activation and increased exodermal lignification that restricts lateral root emergence.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative time-course RNA-seq correlated with pathway and phenotype data
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator candidate for lignin accumulation during the excision response
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root tip excision; putative ABA responsiveness discussed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed among MYBs that may connect ABA/stress signaling to lignin induction.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Editing may alter stress adaptation and other MYB-regulated processes.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Lignin biosynthetic genes (inferred, not directly validated)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively associated with increased expression of lignin biosynthetic genes in Bd21-3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdMYB112 homologs were consistently upregulated in Bd21-3 after root tip excision. 2. This pattern accompanies stronger monolignol pathway induction. 3. Elevated MYB112 expression is linked with exodermal lignification and the fishbone phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Mechanical wounding/root tip excision; putative ABA signaling

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BdMYB54 may mark a contrasting lignification/remodeling program in the permissive accession Bd21; useful for comparative breeding but direct engineering utility remains uncertain without functional validation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BdMYB54 is upregulated in Bd21 rather than Bd21-3 during the excision response, indicating accession-specific regulatory rewiring of secondary wall programs.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not established; accession-biased regulator associated with a more permissive emergence phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Likely secondary cell wall/lignin-related transcriptional control
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported among MYBs differentially induced in Bd21; direct target genes unknown.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unknown due to lack of functional perturbation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation, direction on lignin genes not directly shown
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdMYB54 is induced in Bd21 during the root tip excision response. 2. Its expression differs from the Bd21-3 MYB profile. 3. This supports accession-specific regulatory programs underlying divergent root architecture responses.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BdMYB74 is a comparative regulator candidate distinguishing the permissive accession response; practical use awaits direct functional tests.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BdMYB74 is upregulated in Bd21 during the excision response, contrasting with Bd21-3-biased MYB108/112 induction.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cited as Bd21-upregulated MYB; no direct downstream targets tested.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdMYB74 is induced in Bd21 after root tip excision. 2. Its expression profile differs from the Bd21-3-associated MYB pattern. 3. This suggests distinct regulatory solutions for wall remodeling between accessions.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BdMYB5 may act oppositely to Bd21-3-enriched MYBs and could help define permissive root emergence programs, but current evidence is descriptive only.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BdMYB5 is downregulated in Bd21 during the excision response, indicating accession-specific remodeling of MYB networks related to lignification and emergence.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned only in comparative MYB expression context.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdMYB5 is downregulated in Bd21 after root tip excision. 2. This highlights accession-specific differences in MYB regulatory networks. 3. Its exact role in lignin accumulation or emergence remains unresolved.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BdMYB50 is a comparative candidate in accession-specific wall-remodeling programs, but direct application is premature.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BdMYB50 is downregulated in Bd21 during the excision response; role in the lignification/emergence phenotype is not functionally validated.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in comparative MYB dataset only.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdMYB50 is downregulated in Bd21 after excision. 2. This contributes to the distinct MYB expression landscape between accessions. 3. Functional importance for lignification remains uncertain.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BdMYB94 is a putative upstream stress/ABA-responsive regulator candidate for reducing exodermal lignification and improving root branching plasticity; requires direct validation before deployment.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BdMYB94 is cited as constitutively upregulated in Bd21-3 and may contribute to the altered MYB landscape linked to stress-induced lignification.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Putative upstream ABA-responsive controller of lignin/suberin-associated stress programs, not directly validated
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root tip excision; possible ABA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively upregulated in Bd21-3 according to discussion
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in discussion as ABA-responsive; direct functional role in this paper is inferential.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Inferred positive effect on lignin/suberin-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdMYB94 is discussed as constitutively upregulated in Bd21-3. 2. It is considered potentially ABA-responsive. 3. It may contribute to the regulatory background favoring stress-induced exodermal lignification.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BdMYB91 is a speculative target for rewiring stress-induced barrier programs in roots, pending validation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BdMYB91 is cited as constitutively upregulated in Bd21-3 and may be part of the ABA-linked MYB landscape associated with altered lignification.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Putative condition-responsive regulator, not directly demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root tip excision; possible ABA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively upregulated in Bd21-3 according to discussion
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not functionally tested in this work.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Inferred positive association with stress wall genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdMYB91 is discussed as upregulated in Bd21-3. 2. It may participate in an ABA-responsive regulatory state. 3. Direct causal links to lignification are not shown in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BdMYB93 is of special interest because related MYB93 negatively regulates lateral root emergence; manipulating its expression may alter the emergence checkpoint, but this paper reports downregulation in Bd21-3, so role here may differ from expectation and needs direct testing.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BdMYB93 is downregulated in Bd21-3 despite the inhibited emergence phenotype, indicating a complex regulatory network where exodermal lignification may override canonical lateral-root-emergence regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq; interpretation based on prior literature
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Potential lateral-root-emergence regulator, but not decisive for lignin accumulation in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in Bd21-3 according to discussion
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Lateral root emergence-associated regulatory control; indirect relation to wall remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors cite Arabidopsis literature describing MYB93 as a negative regulator of lateral root emergence; Brachypodium behavior may be species/context-specific.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating MYB93 may alter root branching patterns broadly.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdMYB93 was reported as downregulated in Bd21-3. 2. This contrasts with prior reports of MYB93 as a negative regulator of lateral root emergence in Arabidopsis. 3. The result suggests that Bd21-3 emergence inhibition is primarily linked to exodermal lignification rather than a simple MYB93-like program.
